file_insights.description: Added new 'Recover' CLI command variant and handler for scanning running/ directory
modifications: Added `Recover` variant to `Command` enum with doc comment describing orphan detection
Implemented async handler calling `reaper::recover_orphans()` with colored terminal output
Returns list of recovered card IDs with formatted feedback

patterns_discovered.description: Uses Rust derive macros (Subcommand) for CLI parsing and variant-based dispatch in match expression
Consistent use of `.await?` for async operations with `anyhow::Result<()>` return type
CLI command handler delegates core logic to specialized module (`reaper::recover_orphans()`)
Uses `colors::state_ansi()` helper to colorize output based on card state for visual consistency
Distinguishes between no-results and successful-recovery cases with separate messaging

gotchas_discovered.description: Code calls `reaper::recover_orphans()` which must exist and be properly exported; missing implementation will cause compilation failure
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify reaper module is imported and function signature matches expected async return type

gotchas_discovered.description: Code assumes `.cards/running/` and `.cards/pending/` directories exist; corrupt or missing structures could cause panics
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add defensive checks in recover_orphans() for directory existence and metadata validity

gotchas_discovered.description: Comment mentions 'dead PIDs' detection but implementation is abstracted away; behavior may differ across OS
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document platform-specific behavior and add tests for Linux/macOS compatibility

implementation_strategy: 1. Added `Recover` enum variant with descriptive documentation
2. Implemented handler in match expression with async/await pattern
3. Delegated orphan detection to external `reaper` module
4. Provided graduated user feedback (scanning message → results → summary count)
5. Used consistent color coding for state visualization
approach_outcome.session_efficiency: Direct implementation without revisions; code follows existing patterns in codebase

recommendations.recommendation: Verify `reaper::recover_orphans()` implementation exists and handles all orphan detection criteria (dead PIDs, corrupt meta.json, stale lock files)
Add specific error variants for: missing .cards/ structure, permission denied on rename operations, and metadata parse failures
Create integration tests with fixtures for: (1) empty running/ directory, (2) valid orphaned cards, (3) cards with corrupt meta.json, (4) concurrent dispatcher behavior
Document when 'bop recover' should be used operationally (e.g., after dispatcher crashes, manual intervention scenarios) in CLI help and README
Add debug logging to trace orphan detection criteria and recovery decision logic for operational troubleshooting
